segments.text: [Speaker Notes] Chapter 16, Opener, The ruby-throated hummingbird winters in Central America and migrates to North America for the spring breeding season. These tiny birds must cross the Gulf of Mexico, covering more than 500 miles in a nonstop marathon. Prior to migration, the hummingbirds fatten up, accumulating about 2 g of fat in a total body weight of 4.5 g. The energy needed for a typical 20-hour crossing is supplied by the oxidation of 1.5 g of this stored fat.
